description: Update the public documentation site to cover all new SDK utilities (getAuthCache, setAuthCache, clearAuthCache, fetchText, getMetaContent, buildQueryString, findLocalStorageEntry), add a 'when to use which fetch utility' decision guide, document httpStatusToToolError in the plugin development guide, and add documentation for the homepage and excludePatterns plugin properties.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add new SDK utilities to the utilities reference page
userStories.description: Add documentation for the five new SDK utilities to docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx: getAuthCache/setAuthCache/clearAuthCache (Storage section), fetchText and buildQueryString (Fetch section), getMetaContent (DOM section), findLocalStorageEntry (Storage section).
acceptanceCriteria: Storage section includes getAuthCache, setAuthCache, clearAuthCache with signatures, descriptions, and usage example showing token persistence pattern
Storage section includes findLocalStorageEntry with signature, description, and usage example for prefix-based search
Fetch section includes fetchText with signature, description, and example for fetching diffs/raw text
Fetch section includes buildQueryString with signature, description, and example showing undefined filtering and array support
DOM section includes getMetaContent with signature, description, and example for auth meta tag reading
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/utilities.mdx

Context:
The utilities.mdx page is the comprehensive SDK API reference. It is organized by category: DOM, Fetch, Storage, Page State, Timing, Logging. Each utility has a function signature, description, options table (if applicable), and a code example.

Changes:
Add to the Storage section (after getCookie):
1. `getAuthCache<T>(namespace)` — reads from globalThis.__openTabs.tokenCache. Example: `const token = getAuthCache<string>('discord');`
2. `setAuthCache<T>(namespace, value)` — writes to globalThis.__openTabs.tokenCache. Example: `setAuthCache('jira', { accountId, cloudId });`
3. `clearAuthCache(namespace)` — clears a token. Example: `clearAuthCache('sentry');`
4. `findLocalStorageEntry(predicate)` — searches keys. Example: `findLocalStorageEntry(key => key.includes('auth0'))`

Add to the Fetch section:
5. `fetchText(url, init?)` — like fetchJSON but returns string. Example: `const diff = await fetchText('/api/diff');`
6. `buildQueryString(params)` — builds URL query string. Example: `const qs = buildQueryString({ page: 1, limit: 20, filter: undefined }); // 'page=1&limit=20'`

Add to the DOM section:
7. `getMetaContent(name)` — reads meta tag. Example: `const userId = getMetaContent('user-id');`

Follow the existing documentation style exactly — each utility gets a ### heading, a TypeScript signature code block, a description paragraph, and a usage example code block.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the existing utility documentation — only add new sections
- Follow the exact heading and code block style used by existing utilities on the page

userStories.title: Add fetch utility decision guide and httpStatusToToolError to plugin development guide
userStories.description: Add a 'Choosing the right fetch utility' subsection to the plugin development guide that helps developers understand when to use fetchJSON vs fetchFromPage vs fetchText vs postJSON etc. Also document httpStatusToToolError in the error handling section.
acceptanceCriteria: Plugin development guide has a decision table or flowchart for choosing fetch utilities
The guide explains that credentials:'include' is automatic — developers do not need to add it
httpStatusToToolError is documented in the error handling section with example showing how to use it for custom API wrappers
The guide mentions that all fetch utilities have a 30-second default timeout
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx

Context:
The current plugin development guide shows a tool example that uses raw `fetch` with manual `credentials: 'include'` (around lines 174-191), then introduces fetchJSON/postJSON in a separate section (lines 254-286). There is no guidance on WHEN to use which utility, and httpStatusToToolError is never mentioned in the guide despite being the proper way to handle HTTP errors.

Changes:
1. In the SDK Utilities > Fetch section (around lines 254-286), add a decision table:

| Need | Use |
|------|-----|
| GET + JSON response | `fetchJSON` |
| POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE + JSON | `postJSON` / `putJSON` / `patchJSON` / `deleteJSON` |
| POST + URL-encoded form | `postForm` |
| POST + multipart file upload | `postFormData` |
| GET + text response (diffs, logs) | `fetchText` |
| Custom request handling | `fetchFromPage` (raw Response) |
| Build URL query parameters | `buildQueryString` |

2. Add a note: 'All fetch utilities automatically include session cookies (credentials: include) and a 30-second timeout. You do not need to add these manually.'

3. In the Error Handling section, add httpStatusToToolError documentation:
```typescript
import { fetchFromPage, httpStatusToToolError } from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk';

const response = await fetchFromPage(url);
if (!response.ok) {
  throw httpStatusToToolError(response, `API error for ${url}`);
}
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the existing raw fetch example — it's still valid for context
- The decision table should be a quick reference, not exhaustive documentation (that's in utilities.mdx)

userStories.title: Document homepage and excludePatterns in plugin class reference
userStories.description: Add documentation for the homepage and excludePatterns properties to the plugin class reference page.
acceptanceCriteria: homepage property documented with description and example
excludePatterns property documented with description and example
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx

Context:
The plugin class reference at docs/content/docs/sdk/plugin-class.mdx documents the abstract properties (name, description, urlPatterns, tools, displayName, isReady) but does NOT document the optional properties `homepage` and `excludePatterns`.

From platform/plugin-sdk/src/index.ts:
```typescript
readonly excludePatterns?: string[];
readonly homepage?: string;
```

- `excludePatterns`: Chrome match patterns for URLs that should NOT match. Useful when a broad urlPattern overlaps with another plugin's domain.
- `homepage`: URL to open when no matching tab exists and the user triggers an 'open tab' action from the side panel.

Changes:
Add these as documented optional properties after the urlPatterns section. Include examples:
```typescript
// Exclude admin pages from matching
readonly excludePatterns = ['*://admin.example.com/*'];

// URL to open when no tab is available
readonly homepage = 'https://app.example.com';
```

Constraints:
- Place these after urlPatterns to keep logical grouping
- Mark them clearly as optional
